heading: Interference with cabin or flight crew
label: 46318 Interference with cabin or flight crew
notes: Amendments2018—Subsec. (a). inserted “or sexually” after “physically” in two places and substituted “$35,000” for “$25,000”.Pub. L. 115–254

Effective DateSection applicable only to fiscal years beginning after , see , set out as an Effective Date of 2000 Amendments note under .Sept. 30, 1999section 3 of Pub. L. 106–181section 106 of this title
amendments: Amendments2018—Subsec. (a). inserted “or sexually” after “physically” in two places and substituted “$35,000” for “$25,000”.Pub. L. 115–254

Effective DateSection applicable only to fiscal years beginning after , see , set out as an Effective Date of 2000 Amendments note under .Sept. 30, 1999section 3 of Pub. L. 106–181section 106 of this title

children.heading: .—General Rule
children.label: a .—General Rule
children.text: An individual who physically or sexually assaults or threatens to physically or sexually assault a member of the flight crew or cabin crew of a civil aircraft or any other individual on the aircraft, or takes any action that poses an imminent threat to the safety of the aircraft or other individuals on the aircraft is liable to the United States Government for a civil penalty of not more than $35,000.

children.heading: .—Compromise
children.label: 1 .—Compromise
children.text: The Secretary may compromise the amount of a civil penalty imposed under this section.

children.heading: .—Setoff
children.label: 2 .—Setoff
children.text: The United States Government may deduct the amount of a civil penalty imposed or compromised under this section from amounts the Government owes the person liable for the penalty.
